# Contributing to PocketDrop

:tada: First off, thanks for taking the time to help improve PocketDrop! :tada:

**Please note: PocketDrop is currently developed by a solo maintainer and is not accepting code contributions or pull requests at this time.**

That said, your feedback is still a huge help to the project. Bug reports, feature suggestions, and questions are all warmly welcomed and encouraged.

> [!NOTE]
> If you like the project but just don't have time to contribute, that's okay. There are other easy ways to support it and show your appreciation, which I would also be very happy about:
> - Star the project on Github
> - Tweet or post about it
> - Mention the project to your friends or colleagues

Use the table of contents icon in the top corner of this document to get to a specific section of this guide quickly.


## Table of Contents

- [I Have a Question](#i-have-a-question)
- [Reporting Bugs](#reporting-bugs)
- [Suggesting Enhancements](#suggesting-enhancements)


## I Have a Question

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Before asking a question, please search the existing [Issues](https://github.com/Naofunyan/PocketDrop/issues) to see if it has already been asked.

If you still need clarification:
- Open a new [Issue](https://github.com/Naofunyan/PocketDrop/issues).
- Provide as much context as you can about what you are running into.
- Tell us which version of PocketDrop and Windows you are using.

I'll look into it as soon as possible.


## Reporting Bugs

A good bug report shouldn't leave others needing to chase you for more information. Please investigate carefully, collect as much detail as possible, and describe the issue thoroughly. Completing the steps below in advance will help me fix any potential bug as fast as possible.

- Make sure you are using the latest version.
- **Check for duplicates:** To see if other users have experienced (and potentially already solved) the same issue you are having, check if there is not already a bug report existing for your bug or error in the [bug tracker](https://github.com/naofunyan/PocketDrop/issues?q=label%3Abug).
- **Use the template:** When opening a new issue, select the **🐛 Report a bug or unexpected behavior** template.
- **Be detailed:** Include the exact steps to reproduce the issue, what you expected to happen, and what actually happened.
- **Include visuals:** A screenshot or screen recording can help resolve the issue faster.

> [!CAUTION]
> Never report security vulnerabilities or bugs containing sensitive information in the public issue tracker. Please remove any sensitive information before submitting a report.

Once it's filed:

- I will label the issue accordingly.
- I will attempt to reproduce the issue using the steps you provided. If reproduction steps are missing or unclear, I will ask for them and mark the issue as `needs-repro`. Issues tagged `needs-repro` will not be addressed until they can be reproduced.
- If the issue can be reproduced, it will be addressed as soon as possible.


## Suggesting Enhancements

This section guides you through submitting an enhancement suggestion for PocketDrop, **including completely new features and minor improvements to existing functionality**. Following these guidelines helps me understand your suggestion and find related ones. I'd love to hear your ideas for making PocketDrop even better!

Before suggesting a completely new feature or a minor improvement:
- Make sure that you are using the latest version.
- **Check for duplicates:** Search the [issue tracker](https://github.com/naofunyan/PocketDrop/issues) to see if the enhancement has already been suggested. If it has, feel free to add a comment or a 👍 reaction to the existing issue instead of opening a new one.
- **Use the template:** When opening an issue, select the **💡 Feature request** or **💾 Report an unsupported file format** template.
- **Focus on the "Why":** Explain the workflow problem or limitation you're facing and how the proposed feature would help. Make sure your idea fits within the scope and goals of the project, and make a strong case for why it would benefit the majority of users, not just a small subset.


#### Thanks again for helping make PocketDrop effortless for everyone!
